rulings.Case Title: SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES VS. UNION BANK, N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, April 18, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery; Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel is granted. All objections to Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production are overruled. All objections to the Supplemental Interrogatory are overruled. Defendant's urges as opposition to responding to the discovery the entire action should be before single assignment Judge, and that a motion for a single assignment is pending. On April 3, 2014 defendant's motion for a single assignment was denied by order of the Presiding Judge. The defendant shall respond to all outstanding discovery referenced in the motion within thirty days from the date of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: REGENCY CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC VS. ELAJOU GROUP, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Moiton For Order Requiring Pltf To Post An Undertaking
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 18, 2014, Line 5, CROSS COMPLAINANTs ELAJOU GROUP, LLC and JUMA ELAJOU's Motion For Order Requiring Plaintiff To Post An Undertaking. The motion is denied. Defendant fails to show that it has a reasonable probability of prevailing in this action. Plaintiff is suing over the Starwood transaction and the e-mail communication from Mr. Hill to A10 is irrelevant. In addition, CCP 1030 does not apply to plaintiff. The record before the court indicates that plaintiff resides in California because that is where its principal place of business is located. Defendant is also a LLC and CCP 1030 does not apply to it. rulings.Case Title: ANDRE' PATTERSON VS. MARINELLA GONCALVES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Reinstate Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 431.10-431.70, Ccp 469-475, Ccp 585-587.5
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 18, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFF ANDRE' PATTERSON's Motion To Reinstate Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 431.10-431.70, Ccp 469-475, Ccp 585-587.5. Denied. Plaintiff has not sufficiently demonstrated why the Court should reinstate his Complaint. On October 12, 2012, the Court issued an order sustaining, with 10 days leave to amend the demurrer and motion to strike the Complaint. On March 27, 2013, Defendants served the Order. On July 25, 2013, Plaintiff served Notice of Entry of Order. Plaintiff did not amend his Complaint. On September 17, 2013, the Court granted Defendants' unopposed motion to dismiss, with prejudice, pursuant to CCP § 581(f). rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Reinstate Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 431.10-431.70, 469-475, 585-587.5
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 18, 2014, Line 9, PLAINTIFF ANDRE PATTERSON's Motion To Reinstate Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 431.10-431.70, 469-475, 585-587.5. Denied. The requested relief is not ripe for review as to Defendants Catholic Charities and Marinella Goncalves. Plaintiff's Complaint is active against Defendants Catholic Charities and Marinella Goncalves; reinstatement is not necessary. To the extent that Plaintiff seeks to reinstate his Complaint against Defendants Andrea Milano and Littler Mendelson, the requested relief is improper. On March 11, 2013, the Court granted Defendants Andrea Milano and Littler Mendelson's Special Motion to Strike. The Complaint cannot be reinstated on the grounds sought by Plaintiff. Furthermore, it is unclear whether Plaintiff served these Defendants Andrea Milano's and Littler Mendelson. rulings.Case Title: JI YANG et al VS. HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 18, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant presents a legitimate business reason for the terminations based on the declaration of Dr. Cabigao. The evidence indicates that even though Dr. Cabigao conferred with others, he was the ultimate decisionmaker. Therefore, to avoid summary judgment, plaintiffs must present substantial responsive evidence showing that Dr. Cabigao's reasons were untrue or pretextual. See Hersant v. Department of Social Services (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 997, 1004-1005. Plaintiff fails to meet that burden. The undisputed evidence shows that: (1) Dr. Cabigao is in the same class as plaintiffs (Asian) and there is an inference against racial discrimination in such circumstances; (2) Dr. Cabigao had a role in hiring plaintiff Yang and there is an inference against discrimination where the hirer and firer are the same person; (3) the nurses that replaced plaintiffs are Asian; and (4) seventy-five percent of the nurses that work for defendant are Asian. Lastly, plaintiff contends that defendant acted discriminatorily because Ms. Serafin and Ms. Newman, who treated Resident X, were not terminated or even investigated. The evidence shows that Ms. Serafin and Ms. Newman did not see Resident X as frequently as plaintiffs and plaintiffs' theory of disparate treatment founders because the plaintiffs on the one hand, and Ms. Serafin and Ms. Newman, on the other hand, were not similarly situated. See Wills v. Sup. Crt. (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 143. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: 851 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC VS. NELSON BROTHERS PROFESSIONAL REAL ESTATE, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 18, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT NELSON BROTHERS PROFESSIONAL REAL ESTATE, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact on the one hand, whether Plaintiff breached the parties' agreement and/or the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing by not providing the year end financials for the property to Defendant and on the other hand, whether defendant's performance was excused. The declaration of Beau Jaussi indicates that Plaintiff promised to turnover the financial information, but never did so. There is a factual dispute regarding whether Plaintiff's conduct injured Defendant's right to receive the benefits of the agreement. The promise to provide the financial information does not clearly conflict with an express term of the parties' agreement. In addition, implied covenants may coexist with express covenants where they relate to different subjects. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: 851 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC VS. NELSON BROTHERS PROFESSIONAL REAL ESTATE, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 18, 2014, Line 12, PLAINTIFF 851 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/Adjudication. Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication is denied to plaintiff's causes of action for breach of contract and declaratory judgment. Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication is denied and to defendant's claims in its cross-complaint for breach of contract, breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, declaratory relief, money had and received, and unjust enrichment. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Plaintiff breached the parties' agreement and/or the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing by not providing the year end financials for the property to Defendant. The declaration of Beau Jaussi indicates that Plaintiff promised to turnover the financial information, but never did so. There is a factual dispute regarding whether Plaintiff's conduct injured Defendant's right to receive the benefits of the agreement. The promise to provide the financial information does not clearly conflict with an express term of the parties' agreement. In addition, implied covenants may coexist with express covenants where they relate to different subjects. The Court grants summary adjudication on the fraud and negligent misrepresentation causes of action. First, Defendant is estopped from pleading fraud in the inducement. Paragraph 5 of the cross-complaint alleges that "[i]n the days leading up to the December 27, 2012 Closing Date, Buyer discovered that Seller's Confidential Offering Memorandum contained Multiple material misrepresentations." Defendant agreed to enter into the First Amendment thereafter. "California law has, for more than a century, recognized that a plaintiff claiming to have been induced into signing a contract by fraud or deceit is deemed to have waived a claim of damages arising therefrom if, after discovery of the alleged fraud, he enters into a new contract with the defendant regarding the same subject matter that supersedes the former agreement and confers upon him significant benefits." Oakland Raiders v. Oakland-Alameda County Coliseum, Inc. (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 1175, 1185 (emphasis in original). Second, at page 53 of Mr. Jaussi's deposition, he admitted that Defendant could not justifiably rely on the offering memorandum. Defendant's reference to alleged misrepresentations in late February or March of 2013 are irrelevant because the cross-complaint does not raise those issues. Defendant cannot avoid summary judgment/adjudication by relying on unpleaded theories of liability. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VIA LICENSING CORPORATION VS. BARNES & NOBLE, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Production Of Documents From Barnes & Noble, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, April 18, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF VIA LICENSING CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From Barnes & Noble, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. The underlying contract dispute involves whether defendant's product called a "Nook" fits into the contractual definition of a "Personal Computer" for determination of royalties that are owed. The parties have engaged in an extensive meet and confer effort resulting in a single-category document request which focuses on the defendant's marketing efforts for the product. The defendant agrees "certain marketing documents are relevant" for purposes of contract interpretation. The issue before the Court appears to be whether the language of the final iteration of the document category is overbroad and/or burdensome. What is presently unclear to the Court is in what manner is the category overbroad and the extent of its burden of an electronic production of the documents. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN FACTORS CORP. OF TEXAS VS. ROBERT G. YRANI DBA SAN FRANCISCO GOLD CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 18, 2014, Line 15, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN FACTORS CORP. OF TEXAS' Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK A. MISSUD VS. LUCY ARMENDARIZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 18, 2014, Line 18, DEFENDANTs LUCY ARMENDARIZ and JOANN REMKE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Per Order of the Presiding Judge, this matter is continued to May 9, 2014 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. The parties should be prepared to address the status of Plaintiff's 170.1 challenge on the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)
